title: Ranking Bruins’ top 10 prospects: Who stands out in Boston’s growing pipeline?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Dean Letourneau, and Will Zellers lead the Bruins' top 10 prospects list.
Defenseman Frederic Brunet could push for an NHL role with his improved all-around game.
Goalie Michael DiPietro leads with AHL MVP honors, signaling NHL readiness for 2026-27.

title: One of the few remaining privately owned Berkshire ‘cottages’ sold for $4 million
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Pine Needles, a Colonial Revival estate in Lenox, was sold for $4 million in June.
The 13,261-square-foot home features six bedrooms, eight bathrooms, and unique rooms like the 'Teak Room.'
The estate includes a two-story carriage house, a three-bay garage, and a swimming pool fed by a spring.

title: Body found in river near Brockton water treatment plant, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Body of an unidentified man found in river near Brockton water treatment plant, DA's office reports.
Massachusetts State Police retrieved the body; investigation into the incident is ongoing.
Two bodies had previously been found in the same location in 2024, with no foul play suspected.

title: Man arrested for alleged assault of rideshare passenger at Logan Airport
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Leonard Bacon, 23, from Lowell, was arrested for allegedly assaulting a rideshare passenger at Logan Airport, Boston.
Bacon faces charges of assault and battery with a dangerous weapon causing serious bodily injury, police reported.
The stabbing incident occurred around 5:30 a.m. Friday, and Bacon is to appear in East Boston District Court Monday.

title: WBZ meteorologist returns after taking 3 years away to care for her son with cancer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: WBZ meteorologist Sarah Wroblewski returns after nearly three years off to care for son Declan, who had brain cancer.
Wroblewski announced Declan is out of active treatment and she will provide weather forecasts on weekends and some weekdays.
Declan faces lasting effects from cancer and treatment, but significant progress allows Wroblewski and her husband to return to work.

title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people died in a two-vehicle crash on Route 6 near the Westport-Dartmouth line on Friday evening.
The victims were identified as Tristan Bedient, 34, of Acushnet, and Kate Aldrich, 51, of New Bedford.
The crash involving a Camry and a Saab is under investigation by Massachusetts State Police and local authorities.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit and the union reached a one-year contract, ending a 10-day strike by drivers and mechanics.
The new agreement includes a $4 hourly raise, maintenance of Saturday overtime, and the continuation of fare-free bus service.
Service disruptions affected multiple Massachusetts towns, with normal operations expected to resume despite potential initial delays.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox secured a 3-2 victory over the New York Mets, marking their ninth consecutive win.
Brayan Bello pitched 4 1/3 innings, allowing 2 hits and 1 earned run to help lower his ERA to 6.06.
The Red Sox have won 14 of their last 16 games and allowed two runs or fewer in six straight games.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after his four-night concert run at Fenway Park.
Kahan becomes the fourth artist to join the Hall, alongside Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band.
Highlights of Kahan's Fenway shows included fireworks and appearances from sports figures like Rob Gronkowski and David Ortiz.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from a $45 million settlement with Cash App over alleged deceptive practices.
Cash App allegedly misled users about safety, failed to protect against fraud, and neglected adequate user support.
Settlement requires improved fraud protections, ceasing misleading claims, and establishing reliable customer service by Block Inc.
